title: e: doctor stale .serve.lock advisory + --fix unlink
description: When `fab serve` crashes (or is SIGKILLed), a stale `.fabric/.serve.lock` can remain on disk and block subsequent serve attempts with a confusing error. Add a doctor check that detects a lock file older than N hours (default 24) with no corresponding running pid, surfaces it as a non-blocking advisory in `fab doctor` output, and unlinks it under `fab doctor --fix`.

files.change: Add new check `stale_serve_lock` — read .fabric/.serve.lock, parse pid + ts, verify pid is alive AND ts within 24h; advisory otherwise; --fix unlinks

implementation: Locate the doctor check registry (mirrors rc.22 agents_meta_stale style)
Implement stale-lock detection: parse lock JSON, check ts age + pid liveness via `process.kill(pid, 0)` try/catch
Under --fix: unlink the stale lock file; emit serve_lock_cleared event
Edge case: lock file unparseable / truncated → treat as stale; --fix removes
Add unit tests: (a) fresh lock with live pid → no advisory, (b) stale lock with dead pid → advisory, (c) --fix removes stale lock, (d) malformed lock → treated stale + cleared

criteria: stale_serve_lock check listed in `fab doctor` output when applicable
`fab doctor --fix` unlinks stale lock and emits serve_lock_cleared event
Unit tests pass for four scenarios

rationale.chosen_approach: Advisory + --fix unlink — consistent with rc.22 demote-to-warning pattern; non-destructive default
alternatives_considered: Auto-clear lock on next serve attempt (rejected: hides legitimate concurrent-serve attempts)
rationale.tradeoffs: Users must run `fab doctor --fix` to clear — acceptable explicit step

manual_checks: Kill -9 a fab serve process, run `fab serve` again → expect lock-related error → run `fab doctor --fix` → retry serve succeeds
success_metrics: Lock-clear path covered in dogfood without regression
